questions.text: If 5 kg of sugar costs ₹150, what is the cost of 1 kg of sugar?
questions.explanation: Cost of 1 kg = Total cost ÷ Quantity = 150 ÷ 5 = ₹30.

questions.text: A car travels 60 km in 2 hours. What is its speed in km/h?
questions.explanation: Speed = Distance ÷ Time = 60 ÷ 2 = 30 km/h.

questions.text: If 3 pens cost ₹45, how much will 7 pens cost?
questions.explanation: Cost of 1 pen = 45 ÷ 3 = ₹15; cost of 7 pens = 15 × 7 = ₹105.

questions.text: A worker earns ₹240 for 8 hours of work. What is his wage per hour?
questions.explanation: Wage per hour = Total wage ÷ Hours = 240 ÷ 8 = ₹30.

questions.text: If 4 litres of milk cost ₹100, what is the cost of 10 litres?
questions.explanation: Cost per litre = 100 ÷ 4 = ₹25; cost of 10 litres = 25 × 10 = ₹250.

questions.text: A tap fills a tank in 5 hours. How much time will 2 such taps take to fill the tank?
questions.explanation: Two taps fill twice the amount in the same time, so time = 5 ÷ 2 = 2.5 hours.

questions.text: If 7 metres of cloth costs ₹210, what is the cost of 1 metre?
questions.explanation: Cost per metre = 210 ÷ 7 = ₹30.

questions.text: A man walks 12 km in 3 hours. What is his speed?
questions.explanation: Speed = Distance ÷ Time = 12 ÷ 3 = 4 km/h.

questions.text: If 8 kg of rice costs ₹400, what is the cost of 5 kg?
questions.explanation: Cost per kg = 400 ÷ 8 = ₹50; cost of 5 kg = 50 × 5 = ₹250.

questions.text: A machine produces 100 units in 4 hours. How many units does it produce in 1 hour?
questions.explanation: Units per hour = 100 ÷ 4 = 25 units.

questions.text: If 9 litres of juice costs ₹180, what is the cost of 3 litres?
questions.explanation: Cost per litre = 180 ÷ 9 = ₹20; cost of 3 litres = 20 × 3 = ₹60.

questions.text: A worker completes a task in 6 hours. How long will 3 workers take to complete the same task together?
questions.explanation: Time taken by 3 workers = 6 ÷ 3 = 2 hours.

questions.text: If 5 kg of apples cost ₹250, what is the cost of 2 kg?
questions.explanation: Cost per kg = 250 ÷ 5 = ₹50; cost of 2 kg = 50 × 2 = ₹100.

questions.text: A cyclist covers 45 km in 3 hours. What is his speed?
questions.explanation: Speed = Distance ÷ Time = 45 ÷ 3 = 15 km/h.

questions.text: If 12 pens cost ₹180, what is the cost of 20 pens?
questions.explanation: Cost per pen = 180 ÷ 12 = ₹15; cost of 20 pens = 15 × 20 = ₹300.

questions.text: A tank is filled by a pipe in 8 hours. How much time will 4 such pipes take to fill the tank?
questions.explanation: Time taken by 4 pipes = 8 ÷ 4 = 2 hours.

questions.text: If 15 workers can complete a job in 10 days, how many days will 5 workers take to complete the same job?
questions.explanation: Days taken are inversely proportional to workers: 15×10=5×x ⇒ x=30 days.

questions.text: A car runs 360 km on 30 litres of petrol. How many km can it run on 1 litre?
questions.explanation: Distance per litre = 360 ÷ 30 = 12 km.

questions.text: If 7 kg of sugar costs ₹210, what is the cost of 3.5 kg?
A man earns ₹480 in 12 hours. How much will he earn in 20 hours?
questions.explanation: Earning per hour = 480 ÷ 12 = ₹40; earning in 20 hours = 40 × 20 = ₹800.

questions.text: A shopkeeper buys 15 kg of rice for ₹900. What is the cost of 1 kg of rice?
questions.explanation: Cost of 1 kg rice = Total cost ÷ Total kg = 900 ÷ 15 = ₹60. Correct answer is ₹60, option b.

questions.text: If 8 workers can complete a work in 12 days, how many days will 6 workers take to complete the same work?
questions.explanation: Work is inversely proportional to number of workers. Days × Workers = constant. So, 8 × 12 = 6 × x ⇒ x = (8 × 12)/6 = 16 days.

questions.text: A car covers 240 km in 4 hours. How far will it travel in 7 hours at the same speed?
questions.explanation: Speed = 240 ÷ 4 = 60 km/h. Distance in 7 hours = 60 × 7 = 420 km.

questions.text: A tank can be filled by a pipe in 5 hours. How much of the tank will be filled in 3 hours?
questions.explanation: In 1 hour, 1/5 of tank is filled. In 3 hours, 3 × (1/5) = 3/5 of tank is filled.

questions.text: A man earns ₹450 in 9 days. How much will he earn in 15 days at the same rate?
questions.explanation: Earnings per day = 450 ÷ 9 = ₹50. Earnings in 15 days = 50 × 15 = ₹750.

questions.text: If 12 litres of milk cost ₹480, what is the cost of 5 litres?
questions.explanation: Cost of 1 litre = 480 ÷ 12 = ₹40. Cost of 5 litres = 40 × 5 = ₹200.

questions.text: A train travels 360 km in 6 hours. How long will it take to travel 540 km at the same speed?
questions.explanation: Speed = 360 ÷ 6 = 60 km/h. Time = Distance ÷ Speed = 540 ÷ 60 = 9 hours.

questions.text: A worker can paint 3 walls in 5 hours. How many walls can he paint in 8 hours?
questions.explanation: Walls per hour = 3 ÷ 5 = 0.6. Walls in 8 hours = 0.6 × 8 = 4.8 walls (closest option 4.5).

questions.text: A shop sells 20 kg of sugar for ₹1200. What is the price of 8 kg of sugar?
questions.explanation: Price per kg = 1200 ÷ 20 = ₹60. Price for 8 kg = 60 × 8 = ₹480.

questions.text: A water tank is filled by two pipes. Pipe A fills the tank in 6 hours and Pipe B in 4 hours. How long will both pipes take to fill the tank together?
questions.explanation: Pipe A fills 1/6 tank/hr, Pipe B fills 1/4 tank/hr. Together: 1/6 + 1/4 = 5/12 tank/hr. Time = 1 ÷ (5/12) = 12/5 hours = 2.4 hours = 2 hours 24 minutes.

questions.text: A tank can be filled by two pipes A and B. Pipe A can fill the tank in 6 hours and pipe B in 4 hours. Pipe A is opened for 2 hours and then pipe B is opened along with pipe A. How long will it take to fill the tank completely?
questions.explanation: Pipe A fills 1/6 of the tank in 1 hour, so in 2 hours it fills 2/6 = 1/3 of the tank. Remaining tank = 1 - 1/3 = 2/3. Both pipes together fill (1/6 + 1/4) = (2/12 + 3/12) = 5/12 of the tank in 1 hour. Time to fill remaining tank = (2/3) ÷ (5/12) = (2/3) × (12/5) = 8/5 = 1.6 hours. Total time = 2 + 1.6 = 3.6 hours, approximately 3 hours and 36 minutes. Closest option is 3 hours.

questions.text: A worker completes a piece of work in 15 days. After working for 5 days, he is assisted by another worker who can do the same work in 20 days. How many days will they take to complete the remaining work together?
questions.explanation: Work done by first worker in 5 days = 5/15 = 1/3. Remaining work = 1 - 1/3 = 2/3. Work rate of first worker = 1/15 per day; second worker = 1/20 per day. Combined rate = 1/15 + 1/20 = (4/60 + 3/60) = 7/60 per day. Time to finish remaining work = (2/3) ÷ (7/60) = (2/3) × (60/7) = 40/7 ≈ 5.71 days, approximately 6 days.

questions.text: A shopkeeper buys 15 kg of sugar at ₹40 per kg and 25 kg of sugar at ₹45 per kg. He mixes both and sells the mixture at ₹44 per kg. Find his profit or loss percentage.
questions.explanation: Cost price of 15 kg = 15 × 40 = ₹600; 25 kg = 25 × 45 = ₹1125. Total cost = 600 + 1125 = ₹1725. Total weight = 15 + 25 = 40 kg. Cost price per kg = 1725/40 = ₹43.125. Selling price per kg = ₹44. Profit per kg = 44 - 43.125 = ₹0.875. Profit % = (0.875 / 43.125) × 100 ≈ 2.03%, closest to 2.5% profit.

questions.text: A man travels from town A to town B at 40 km/h and returns from B to A at 60 km/h. Find his average speed for the whole journey.
questions.explanation: Average speed for round trip = 2 × speed1 × speed2 / (speed1 + speed2) = 2 × 40 × 60 / (40 + 60) = 4800 / 100 = 48 km/h.

questions.text: A tank can be filled by pipe A in 8 hours and emptied by pipe B in 12 hours. If both pipes are opened simultaneously, how long will it take to fill the tank?
questions.explanation: Filling rate of A = 1/8 per hour; emptying rate of B = 1/12 per hour. Net filling rate = 1/8 - 1/12 = (3 - 2)/24 = 1/24 per hour. Time to fill tank = 1 ÷ (1/24) = 24 hours. However, since B empties, the net rate is slower. Actually, 1/8 - 1/12 = 1/24, so time is 24 hours. But 24 hours is option (a), so correct is 24 hours.

questions.text: A man earns ₹2400 in 12 days working 8 hours a day. How much will he earn in 15 days working 10 hours a day?
questions.explanation: Daily earning for 8 hours = ₹2400 / 12 = ₹200. Hourly earning = ₹200 / 8 = ₹25. For 15 days working 10 hours: total hours = 15 × 10 = 150. Earnings = 150 × 25 = ₹3750.

questions.text: A shopkeeper sells 15 kg of rice for ₹900. Using the unitary method, find the cost of 25 kg of rice.
questions.explanation: Cost of 1 kg rice = ₹900 ÷ 15 = ₹60. Cost of 25 kg = 25 × ₹60 = ₹1500.

questions.text: If a car travels 180 km in 3 hours, find the distance it will travel in 7 hours at the same speed.
questions.explanation: Distance travelled in 1 hour = 180 ÷ 3 = 60 km. Distance in 7 hours = 7 × 60 = 420 km.

questions.text: A worker earns ₹480 in 8 days. How much will he earn in 15 days?
questions.explanation: Earning per day = ₹480 ÷ 8 = ₹60. Earnings in 15 days = 15 × ₹60 = ₹900.

questions.text: A tank can be filled by a pipe in 5 hours. How long will it take to fill the tank if 3 such pipes are opened together?
questions.explanation: Filling rate of 1 pipe = 1 tank ÷ 5 hours = 1/5 tank/hour. For 3 pipes, rate = 3 × 1/5 = 3/5 tank/hour. Time taken = 1 ÷ (3/5) = 5/3 hours = 1 hour 40 minutes.

questions.text: A man can complete a piece of work in 12 days. Another man can do the same work in 8 days. How long will they take to finish the work if they work together?
questions.explanation: Work done by first man in 1 day = 1/12. Second man in 1 day = 1/8. Together in 1 day = 1/12 + 1/8 = (2 + 3)/24 = 5/24. Time to finish work = 1 ÷ (5/24) = 24/5 = 4.8 days = 4 days 16 hours.

questions.text: A shopkeeper buys 40 litres of milk for ₹600. What is the cost of 1 litre? If he sells 1 litre for ₹20, what is his profit or loss?
questions.correct_answer: Cost per litre = ₹15; Profit = ₹5 per litre
accepted_forms: Cost per litre = ₹15; Profit = ₹5 per litre
questions.explanation: Cost per litre = ₹600 ÷ 40 = ₹15. Selling price = ₹20. Profit = ₹20 - ₹15 = ₹5 per litre.

questions.text: A train travels 360 km in 6 hours. How far will it travel in 45 minutes at the same speed?
questions.explanation: Speed = 360 ÷ 6 = 60 km/h. 45 minutes = 0.75 hours. Distance = 60 × 0.75 = 45 km.

questions.text: A tank is filled by a pipe in 4 hours. How much of the tank will be filled in 30 minutes?
questions.explanation: Filling rate = 1 tank ÷ 4 hours = 1/4 tank/hour. In 30 minutes (0.5 hours) filled = 1/4 × 0.5 = 1/8 of the tank.

questions.text: A man walks 12 km in 3 hours. How long will he take to walk 20 km at the same speed?
questions.explanation: Speed = 12 ÷ 3 = 4 km/h. Time for 20 km = 20 ÷ 4 = 5 hours.

questions.text: A shopkeeper sells 5 pens for ₹60. Using the unitary method, find the cost of 8 pens.
questions.explanation: Cost of 1 pen = ₹60 ÷ 5 = ₹12. Cost of 8 pens = 8 × ₹12 = ₹96.

questions.case_text: Ravi runs a small shop in Mumbai where he sells sugar. He buys sugar in bulk at ₹360 for 12 kilograms. He wants to sell the sugar in smaller packets to his customers.

subparts.text: What is the cost of 1 kilogram of sugar?
subparts.explanation: Cost of 1 kg = Total cost ÷ total kg = 360 ÷ 12 = ₹30.

subparts.text: If Ravi sells 5 kilograms of sugar, how much will he charge?
subparts.explanation: Cost for 5 kg = cost of 1 kg × 5 = 30 × 5 = ₹150.

subparts.text: Ravi wants to make a packet of 2.5 kilograms of sugar. How much should he charge for this packet?

questions.case_text: Anjali fills a water tank that can hold 500 litres. She uses a pipe that fills 50 litres of water in 10 minutes.

subparts.text: How many litres of water does the pipe fill in 1 minute?
subparts.explanation: Water filled in 1 minute = 50 litres ÷ 10 = 5 litres.

subparts.text: How long will it take to fill the entire 500-litre tank?
subparts.explanation: Time = Total litres ÷ litres per minute = 500 ÷ 5 = 100 minutes.

subparts.text: If Anjali wants to fill only 250 litres, how many minutes will it take?
